growth_records.cite: Qin et al. Alternative strategies of nutrient acquisition and energy conservation map to the biogeography of marine ammonia-oxidizing archaea. The ISME Journal (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-0710-7. [PMC7490402]
growth_records.snippet: the average generation time of SCM1 culture has decreased from ~2 days in 2007 to ~1 day in 2011 and stabilized at around 1 day by 2018
growth_records.notes: Generation (doubling) time reported as ~2 days for the ancestral 2007 SCM1 culture. Converted to hours (2*24=48 h) then mu=ln2/48=0.01444 h^-1. N. maritimus SCM1 is an aerobic ammonia-oxidizing archaeon (chemolithoautotroph); temperature not stated in main text for this culture so left null (avoided assuming the commonly-used ~30°C from the original isolation paper, which is not this paper).

growth_records.medium: liquid mineral medium (composition detailed in supplementary information, not in main text)
growth_records.cite: Qin et al. Alternative strategies of nutrient acquisition and energy conservation map to the biogeography of marine ammonia-oxidizing archaea. The ISME Journal (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-0710-7. [PMC7490402]
growth_records.snippet: the average generation time of SCM1 culture has decreased from ~2 days in 2007 to ~1 day in 2011 and stabilized at around 1 day by 2018
growth_records.notes: Generation (doubling) time reported as ~1 day, stabilized by 2018 after >3000 generations of continuous lab passage (attributed in part to a mutation increasing growth rate). Converted: doubling_time_h = 1*24=24h; mu=ln2/24=0.02888 h^-1. Distinct observation from the 2007 ancestral culture (same strain lineage, different passage history/generation time).

growth_records.medium: SCM medium (Konneke et al., ref.31), Chelex-treated, EDTA-buffered
growth_records.cite: Shafiee et al. Iron requirements and uptake strategies of the globally abundant marine ammonia-oxidising archaeon, Nitrosopumilus maritimus SCM1. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0434-8. [PMC6776035]
growth_records.snippet: Maximum specific growth rates, µmax, were observed in 7550 pmol L−1 Fe′ treatments... µ (d−1) 0.51 (±0.01), µ/µmax 1 (±0.02)
growth_records.notes: mu_max reported as 0.51 d-1 (±0.01) at 7550 pmol/L Fe' (Table 1). Converted to h-1: 0.51/24=0.02125 h-1. Doubling time calculated as ln2/mu = 32.6 h. Oxygen status (aerobic) inferred from known obligate-aerobic ammonia-oxidizing physiology of N. maritimus; not explicitly restated in this paper's text.

growth_records.cite: Shafiee et al. Iron requirements and uptake strategies of the globally abundant marine ammonia-oxidising archaeon, Nitrosopumilus maritimus SCM1. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0434-8. [PMC6776035]
growth_records.notes: mu reported as 0.50 d-1 at 1050 pmol/L Fe' (Table 1). Converted: mu_h=0.50/24=0.02083 h-1; doubling time=ln2/mu_h=33.3 h.

up.method: calculated: steady-state uptake rate rho_ss = Q x mu (Q=cellular Fe quota measured by ICP-MS, mu=specific growth rate), computed jointly from the 300 and 550 pmol/L Fe' treatments (growing at 81% and 93% of mu_max)

growth_records.cite: Shafiee et al. Iron requirements and uptake strategies of the globally abundant marine ammonia-oxidising archaeon, Nitrosopumilus maritimus SCM1. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0434-8. [PMC6776035]
growth_records.snippet: Fe′ uptake rate was 1.1 × 10−22 mol Fe cell−1 h−1 (±3.1 × 10−23) and the Fe′ uptake rate constant, Kin... was 4.5 × 10−13 L cell−1 h−1 (±1.2 × 10−13)
growth_records.notes: mu=0.47 d-1(±0.02) at 550 pmol/L Fe' (Table 1) -> mu_h=0.47/24=0.01958 h-1. rho_ss and Kin (=rho_ss/[Fe']=4.5e-13 L cell-1 h-1) were jointly derived by the authors from cellular Fe quota (Table 1) and mu at the 300 and 550 pmol/L Fe' treatments using rho_ss=Q*mu; reported as a single combined value for both treatments, attached here to the 550 pmol/L record.

up.method: calculated: rho_ss=Q x mu jointly from 300 & 550 pmol/L Fe' treatments (see companion 550 pmol/L record for detail)

growth_records.cite: Shafiee et al. Iron requirements and uptake strategies of the globally abundant marine ammonia-oxidising archaeon, Nitrosopumilus maritimus SCM1. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0434-8. [PMC6776035]

Shafiee et al. Iron requirements and uptake strategies of the globally abundant marine ammonia-oxidising archaeon, Nitrosopumilus maritimus SCM1. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0434-8. [PMC6776035]
growth_records.snippet: Cultures treated with 250 pmol L-1 Fe′ grew at the lowest µ (27% of µmax)... µ (d-1) 0.13 (±0.05)
growth_records.notes: mu=0.13 d-1(±0.05) reported (Table 1) at growth-limiting 250 pmol/L Fe'; mu_h=0.13/24=0.005417 h-1, doubling=ln2/mu_h=127.9h.

growth_records.medium: SCM medium, DFB-buffered (siderophore-bound Fe), 1050 nmol/L FeDFB

up.compound: FeDFB (Fe bound to siderophore desferrioxamine B)
up.units: L cell-1 h-1 (as printed in source; internal consistency check (rate/[FeDFB]=Kin) confirms rate is intended as mol Fe cell-1 h-1)
up.method: calculated: rho_ss = Q x mu at 1050 nmol/L FeDFB treatment; Kin = rho_ss/[FeDFB]

growth_records.cite: Shafiee et al. Iron requirements and uptake strategies of the globally abundant marine ammonia-oxidising archaeon, Nitrosopumilus maritimus SCM1. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0434-8. [PMC6776035]
growth_records.snippet: Only treatments with 1050 nmol L−1 FeDFB displayed SCM1 growth, reaching µ of 0.44 d−1... this is significantly lower than with EDTA-buffered treatments...which reached µ of 0.50 d−1
growth_records.notes: mu=0.44 d-1 reported for growth on siderophore-bound Fe (negligible free Fe', [Fe']=5.2e-9 pmol/L calculated to be negligible); mu_h=0.44/24=0.01833 h-1, doubling=ln2/mu_h=37.8h. FeDFB uptake rate and Kin as directly stated in text; flagged medium confidence because the paper prints both the raw uptake rate and the derived rate constant with the same units (L cell-1 h-1), which is dimensionally inconsistent with the stated formula rho_ss=Q*mu (mol Fe cell-1 h-1); likely a units typo in the original paper, values transcribed verbatim.

growth_records.cite: Meador et al. Carbon recycling efficiency and phosphate turnover by marine nitrifying archaea. Science Advances (2020). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.aba1799. [PMC7209981]
growth_records.snippet: SCM1 ... Initial Pi 910 nM: tau_PO4 661+/-24 h, C0/Nn 0.91+/-0.06 mol mol-1, DOC0/Nn 43+/-14 mmol mol-1, PER-DOC 4.7+/-2 %, P0/Nn 1.2+/-0.1 mmol mol-1, DOP0/Nn 20+/-3 umol mol-1, PER-DOP 1.6+/-0.2 % (Table 1).
growth_records.notes: Aquarium strain SCM1, P-replete (initial Pi 910 nM > 0.4 uM threshold). Yields are per-condition Table 1 values (reported directly, no arithmetic). C0/Nn = mol C fixed per mol NH3 nitrified; P0/Nn = mmol P assimilated per mol N; DOC0/Nn & DOP0/Nn = DOC/DOP released per mol N; PER-DOC/PER-DOP = % of assimilated C/P released as dissolved organics. Volumetric C fixation ~1.5 umol C L-1 h-1 under P-replete (text). Full SCM recipe cited to ref 15 (Konneke) not reproduced in paper -> medium_composition_stated:false; only stated amendments (1 mM ammonium; Pi per condition) listed. pH not stated. No explicit growth rate (mu)/doubling time reported (exponential phase referenced only).

up.units: volumetric ammonia-oxidation rate in nM d-1 (Fig. 1m); kinetic Vmax(NH3) and Km(NH3) in Supplementary Tables 4-5
up.method: 15N-NH4+ (98 atom%) tracer; rate from accumulation of 15N in the NOx- product pool relative to initial pool; NH4+ by fluorometric method (0.7 nM detection limit)

sec.units: nM d-1 (stoichiometric product of NH3 oxidation)
sec.method: NOx- accumulation quantified by Continuous Flow AA3 auto-analyzer (30 nM detection limit) and 15N enrichment via denitrifier method (GC-IRMS)

growth_records.cite: Tong, Senwei et al. Ammonia oxidizers offset acidification stress via adaptive substrate affinity in aquatic ecosystems. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-68747-z. [PMC12954068]
growth_records.snippet: Nitrosopumilus maritimus strain SCM1 was cultured in HEPES buffered synthetic Crenarchaeota medium (SCM) at 30 degrees C in the dark. Cultures in the late exponential phase were transferred to fresh SCM medium (1% of inoculum) without the a
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (archaeon) pure-culture experimental substrate-uptake condition: ammonia oxidation = NH3 consumption -> NO2- production, measured by 15N-NH4+ tracer accumulation in NOx-. Numeric rate values are NOT in the main-text XML: volumetric AOR for SCM1 appear only in Fig. 1m, and the fitted kinetic parameters Vmax(NH3), Km(NH3) and affinity alpha(NH3) are only in Supplementary Tables 4-5 and Fig. 2 (0 inline <table> elements in this XML) -> rate fields left null rather than invented. Only text-stated quantitative anchors: overall across-study AOR range 0.9-376 nM d-1 (spans ALL field stations + culture, NOT SCM1-specific, so not assigned here); per-0.1-unit-pH-drop AOA pure-culture responses Km(NH3) -19%+/-7%, alpha(NH3) +94%+/-7%, Vmax -9.0%+/-0.1% (these are normalized acidification sensitivities, not absolute rates). Medium: SCM composition not given in this paper (cited to refs 11,41); marine autotroph, HEPES-buffered, NH4+ as sole energy substrate, CO2/bicarbonate as C source. pH: control plus an acidified gradient (values not stated numerically in main text). No biomass, growth rate, doubling time or yields reported -> qS in mmol/gDW/h not derivable. Volumetric-only, not biomass-normalized. Other three papers in this batch yielded no records: PMC12929690 (host macrophage immunometabolism, U-13C-glucose tracing on mouse cells not the bacterium P. aeruginosa PAO1); PMC12932662 (mouse diet/gut-microbiota behaviour; B. longum APC1472 only grown to OD600 1.1-1.2 ~1e9 CFU/ml as inoculum, no rates); PMC12949522 (FEMS Microbiology Reviews review article, no primary data).

up.units: fmol N cell-1 d-1 (Vmax; per-cell, not per-gDW)
up.method: substrate-dependent kinetics (Michaelis-Menten); ammonia oxidation to NO2- Vmax equals NO2- production Vmax at 1:1 stoichiometry; Km(app)=132 nmol/L NH4+ for ammonia
O2 uptake apparent half-saturation Km(app)=133 nmol/L (NH4+ equivalent); O2 is primary source of O atoms in N2O

sec.method: substrate-dependent Michaelis-Menten kinetics; Km(app)=220 +/- 50 nmol/L NH4+
15N/18O dual-isotope labeling incubations; Michaelis-Menten Vmax; >3 orders of magnitude lower than NO2- production

mfa.note: Vmax; dominant N flux; Km(app) 220 nmol/L
Vmax; trace side product; hybrid formation + hydroxylamine oxidation pathways; nitrite reduction insignificant

yields.product_yields: N2O yield ~1.4e-4 of NO2- (0.97 amol / 7.14 fmol N cell-1 d-1 = 0.00014 mol N2O-N per mol NO2--N; derived from the two reported Vmax values)

growth_records.cite: Wan, Xianhui S. et al. Pathways of N2O production by marine ammonia-oxidizing archaea determined from dual-isotope labeling. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2220697120. [PMC10243131]
growth_records.method: not reported (no specific growth rate given; per-cell metabolic rates reported instead)
growth_records.snippet: the maximum rate (V max) for N2O production (0.97 +/- 0.02 amol N cell-1 d-1) was more than three orders of magnitude lower than that for NO2- production (7.14 +/- 0.25 fmol N cell-1 d-1)... Km(app) for NO2- production (220 +/- 50 nmol L-1
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote = marine ammonia-oxidizing archaeon N. maritimus SCM1. Rates are CELL-SPECIFIC (per cell per day), NOT per gDW and NOT tied to a reported growth rate, so cannot be converted to mmol/gDW/h without biomass-per-cell data (not given). Measured Vmax: ammonia oxidation = nitrite production = 7.14 +/- 0.25 fmol N cell-1 d-1 (this single flux serves as both NH4+ uptake and NO2- secretion, 1:1 N stoichiometry); N2O production Vmax = 0.97 +/- 0.02 amol N cell-1 d-1. Apparent half-saturation Km(app): NO2- production 220 +/- 50 nmol/L NH4+; ammonia 132 nmol/L; O2 uptake 133 nmol/L (last two cited as previously determined for SCM1). N2O:NO2- Vmax ratio = 0.97 amol / 7.14 fmol = 0.97e-18 / 7.14e-15 = 1.36e-4 (derived arithmetic). Conditions: SCM medium, pH 7.8, 30 C, dark, batch, mid-late exponential phase. This is a kinetics/isotope paper, not a GEM/biomass-yield study; no growth rate, yield, or per-gDW flux reported.
